zh.lifeSummary: 當Adalbert I. Herzog von Zähringen, später Herzog von Teck於大約1135年出生在德國巴登可萊斯 福包格亞林根時，他的父親Conrad I von Zähringen44歲，母親Cleménce de Luxembourg- Namur26歲。他於1155年與Adelheid Přemyslova在德國爾特波格吉海姆吉海姆 武特 特可結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1195年9月19日在德國爾特波格奧拉赤 蒂賓根度斯林根波格享年61歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Adalbert I. Herzog von Zähringen, später Herzog von Teck于大约1135年出生在德国巴登可萊斯 福包格亞林根时，他的父亲Conrad I von Zähringen44岁，母亲Cleménce de Luxembourg- Namur26岁。他于1155年与Adelheid Přemyslova在德国爾特波格吉海姆吉海姆 武特 特可结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1195年9月19日在德国爾特波格奧拉赤 蒂賓根度斯林根波格享年61岁。

nameMeanings.name: Herzog
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Erwin, Fritz, Heinz, Kurt, Otto, Wolfgang, Franz, Arno, Ernst, Hildegarde, Reinhold.

German: from the Middle High German title of nobility herzoge ‘duke’ (Old High German herizoho, from heri ‘army’ + ziohan ‘to lead’, a calque of the Byzantine title stratēlatēs ‘general, commander’, from Greek stratos ‘army’ + elaunein ‘to lead’). The name is unlikely to refer to descent from an actual duke; it is normally an occupational name for the servant of a duke or a nickname for one who put on the airs and graces of a duke. This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Poland, Czechia, and Slovenia (see also 3 below). Compare Hertzog .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): artificial name from German Herzog ‘duke’ (see 1 above). Compare Graf and Kaiser .

variants.name: Hartsook
meanings: Americanized form of German   Herzog  .

variants.name: Herceg
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Croatian Stanko.

Croatian: nickname from herceg ‘duke’, a title of the medieval feudal lords of Herzegovina (‘duke's land’, a region in Bosnia and Herzegovina), derived from the Hungarian form of German Herzog . Compare Erceg .

variants.name: Herczeg
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Hungarian Imre, Laszlo, Tibor, Attila, Ferenc, Gaza, Istvan, Janos.
Hungarian form of German   Herzog  .

variants.name: Herzig
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erna, Fritzi, Hans, Horst, Markus, Monika.
German (eastern and central Germany and Silesia, Switzerland): variant of   Herzog  .
German: from a short form of any of several ancient Germanic personal names composed with hard ‘strong, hardy’, for instance Herto.

variants.name: Hertzog
meanings: German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): variant of Herzog and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this. The surname Hertzog is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine).

variants.name: Hartzog
meanings: Americanized form of German   Herzog   1.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): variant of   Herzog  .

variants.name: Hartsock
meanings: Probably an altered form of   Herzog  .

variants.name: Hartsoe
meanings: Perhaps an altered form of   Herzog  .

variants.name: Hartsough
meanings: Americanized form of German   Herzog  .

nameMeanings.name: von
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Altered form of Welsh   Vaughan  .
Chinese: variant of   Wong  .
Cambodian: written វ៉ុន, unexplained.

variants.name: Vaughan
meanings: Welsh: from fychan, a lenited form of bychan, a diminutive of bach ‘little’. This was often used to distinguish the younger of two bearers of the same personal name, typically the son of a father with the same name.

nameMeanings.name: später
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English:

occupational name for someone who lays slates on roofs, from Middle English sclat(t)er, occasionally slater ‘slater’ (a derivative of Old French esclate ‘slate’ + the Middle English agent suffix -er). See also Slate .

in Sussex and adjacent counties, Slater and Slatter are probably post-medieval pronunciations of Slaughter ; there seems to be no medieval evidence in the southern coast counties for the occupational name Slater in 1 above.

variants.name: Slayter
meanings: English (Middlesex and Buckinghamshire): variant of   Slater  .

variants.name: Slatter
meanings: English: variant of   Slater  .
Americanized form of Swiss German   Schlatter  .

variants.name: Slate
meanings: English: occupational or nickname perhaps for a slater or roofer, or a supplier of slates, from Middle English s(c)lat(e) ‘slate’ (Old French esclat). See Slater .

variants.name: Slaughter
meanings: English:

in East Anglia and Essex, an occupational name from Middle English slaughter ‘butcher’, a derivative of Middle English slaught ‘butchery’ + er, or from a shortened form of the synonymous Middle English slaughterer, a derivative of slaughter ‘butchery’ + -er. Compare Slater 2.

in Sussex and Surrey a habitational name denoting residence at one or other of several minor placenames such as Slaughter Bridge in Slinfold, Slaughter Bridge in Shipley, Slaughterford (Farm) in Itchingfield, the lost Slaughters in Billingshurst (all Sussex), and Slaughterwicks Barn in Charlwood (Surrey). The names may derive from Middle English slo(gh) ‘sloe, blackthorn’ (Old English slāh) + tre ‘tree’ (Old English trēow), or from Middle English sloghtre, sloghtere ‘slough, mire, muddy place’, or perhaps ‘deep river valley’, or ‘ditch’ (Old English slōhtre). The latter is certainly the etymology of Upper and Lower Slaughter (Gloucestershire) and The Slaughter in English Bicknor (Gloucestershire).

nameMeanings.name: Herzog
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Erwin, Fritz, Heinz, Kurt, Otto, Wolfgang, Franz, Arno, Ernst, Hildegarde, Reinhold.

German: from the Middle High German title of nobility herzoge ‘duke’ (Old High German herizoho, from heri ‘army’ + ziohan ‘to lead’, a calque of the Byzantine title stratēlatēs ‘general, commander’, from Greek stratos ‘army’ + elaunein ‘to lead’). The name is unlikely to refer to descent from an actual duke; it is normally an occupational name for the servant of a duke or a nickname for one who put on the airs and graces of a duke. This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Poland, Czechia, and Slovenia (see also 3 below). Compare Hertzog .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): artificial name from German Herzog ‘duke’ (see 1 above). Compare Graf and Kaiser .

meanings: Welsh: from fychan, a lenited form of bychan, a diminutive of bach ‘little’. This was often used to distinguish the younger of two bearers of the same personal name, typically the son of a father with the same name.

nameMeanings.name: Teck
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Dietmar, Jutta.
German (of Slavic origin): from a short form of any of various Slavic personal names composed with the element teh ‘comfort, consolation’ (from the root of the Old Slavic word utěha), e.g. Vojtech .

variants.name: Vojtech
meanings: Czech (Vojtěch) and Slovak: from the personal name Czech Vojtěch, Slovak Vojtech, composed of the Slavic elements voj (from Old Slavic vojъ ‘army’) + teh (from the root of Old Slavic utěha ‘comfort, consolation’), meaning literally ‘consolation of the army’ and figuratively ‘strengthening of the army’. This was a popular personal name among Christians in Eastern Europe, mainly because of the cult of Saint Vojtěch; its Polish form is Wojciech.

History: Saint Vojtěch (c. 956–997) was a bishop of Prague from 982 onward. In 995 he was expelled from Bohemia and in 996 he went to Poland on a mission to the Prussians (members of a heathen Baltic-speaking people, not the German speakers who later took their name). He was killed by the Prussians in 997, and was canonized in 999. He is regarded as the first Polish saint. In German he is known as Saint Adalbert (or Albert) of Prague.

en.lifeSummary: When Adalbert I. Herzog von Zähringen, später Herzog von Teck was born about 1135, in Zähringen, Kreis Freiburg, Baden, Germany, his father, Conrad I von Zähringen, was 44 and his mother, Cleménce de Luxembourg- Namur, was 26. He married Adelheid Přemyslova in 1155, in Kirchheim unter Teck, Kirchheim, Württemberg, Germany. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. He died on 19 September 1195, in Burg, Dußlingen, Tübingen, Württemberg, Germany, at the age of 61.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Adalbert I. Herzog von Zähringen, später Herzog von Teck est né environ 1135, à Zähringen, Kreis Freiburg, Baden, Allemagne, son père, Conrad I von Zähringen, avait 44 ans et sa mère, Cleménce de Luxembourg- Namur, avait 26 ans. Il a épousé Adelheid Přemyslova en 1155, à Kirchheim unter Teck, Kirchheim, Württemberg, Allemagne. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé le 19 septembre 1195, à Burg, Dußlingen, Oberamt Tübingen, Württemberg, Allemagne, à l’âge de 61 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Adalbert I. Herzog von Zähringen, später Herzog von Teck um 1135 in Zähringen, Kreis Freiburg, Baden, Deutschland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Conrad I von Zähringen 44 und seine Mutter Cleménce de Luxembourg- Namur 26. Er hat 1155 in Kirchheim unter Teck, Kirchheim, Württemberg, Deutschland Adelheid Přemyslova geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter. Er starb am 19. September 1195 im Alter von 61 Jahren in Burg, Dußlingen, Oberamt Tübingen, Württemberg, Deutschland.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Adalbert I. Herzog von Zähringen, später Herzog von Teck è nato il circa 1135 nel Zähringen, Kreis Freiburg, Baden, Germania, il suo padre, Conrad I von Zähringen, aveva 44 anni e il suo madre, Cleménce de Luxembourg- Namur, aveva 26 anni. Lui sposato Adelheid Přemyslova nel 1155, nel Kirchheim unter Teck, Kirchheim, Württemberg, Germania. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 2 figlie. Morì il 19 settembre 1195, nel Burg, Dußlingen, Oberamt Tübingen, Württemberg, Germania, all'età di 61 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Adalbert I. Herzog von Zähringen, später Herzog von Teckが約1135年にドイツバデンクレイス フレイブルグズahリンジェンで生まれた時，父Conrad I von Zähringenは44歳，母Cleménce de Luxembourg- Namurは26歳であった。1155年にドイツウuルッテンバーグキルチヘイムキルチヘイム ウンター テックでAdelheid Přemyslovaと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1195年9月19日にドイツウuルッテンバーグオバーアムト トuビンジェンダssリンジェンブルグで61歳で死去した。

ko.Kirchheim, Kirchheim unter Teck, Kirchhe: 독일 wu르테버그 커ch하임 커ch하임 운터 테크 커ch하임

ko.lifeSummary: Adalbert I. Herzog von Zähringen, später Herzog von Teck(이)가 대략 1135년 독일 바든 크레이스 프레이버그 즈ah링에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Conrad I von Zähringen(은)는 44세였고, 그의 어머니 Cleménce de Luxembourg- Namur(은)는 26세였다. 그는 1155년 독일 wu르테버그 커ch하임 커ch하임 운터 테크에서 Adelheid Přemyslova(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1195년 9월 19일 독일 wu르테버그 오베램트 트u빙 드ss링 버그에서 61세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Adalbert I. Herzog von Zähringen, später Herzog von Teck nasceu aproximadamente 1135, em Zähringen, Kreis Freiburg, Baden, Alemanha, seu pai, Conrad I von Zähringen, tinha 44 anos e sua mãe, Cleménce de Luxembourg- Namur, tinha 26 anos. Ele casou-se com Adelheid Přemyslova em 1155, em Kirchheim unter Teck, Kirchheim, Württemberg, Alemanha. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 19 de setembro de 1195, em Burg, Dußlingen, Oberamt Tübingen, Württemberg, Alemanha, com 61 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Adalbert I. Herzog von Zähringen, später Herzog von Teck родился около 1135 г. в местности зaхринген, крис фрибург, баден, Германия, его отцу по имени Conrad I von Zähringen было 44 года, а матери, которую звали Cleménce de Luxembourg- Namur, 26. Он женился на женщине по имени Adelheid Přemyslova в 1155 г. в местности кирчхим унтир текк, кирчхим, вuрттембирг, Германия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. Он умер 19 сентября 1195 г. в местности бург, дуssлинген, обирамт тuбинген, вuрттембирг, Германия в возрасте 61 год.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Adalbert I. Herzog von Zähringen, später Herzog von Teck nació aproximadamente 1135, en Zähringen, Kreis Freiburg, Baden, Alemania, su papá, Conrad I von Zähringen, tenía 44 años y su mamá, Cleménce de Luxembourg- Namur, tenía 26 años. Se casó con Adelheid Přemyslova en 1155, en Kirchheim unter Teck, Kirchheim, Württemberg, Alemania. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 2 hijas. Murió el 19 de septiembre de 1195, en Burg, Dußlingen, Oberamt Tübingen, Württemberg, Alemania, a la edad de 61 años.
